title: Erdős Problem 64
summary: A partial proof with exact Mersenne returns, lexicographic minimal selection, CT2 criticality and bridge contraction, CT3 boundaried uncompressibility, an HSS-forced induced-P13 CT1 stage, a routed maximum-packing CT12 remainder stage, the complete CT10 P13 attachment-label algebra, a polynomial ordered CT6 degree-surplus ledger with exact per-slot activation, the CT15 baseline and free/blocked pair-response stages, the exact CT9 capacity-token partition, and the exact 25-role coupled overload/class decision, together with the framework-native local-port and Type B ledgers retained by the same selected graph.

workflows.title: Verified Erdős 64 prefix
workflows.summary: Inspect the exact Mersenne target algebra, lexicographic minimal selection, CT2 criticality and bridge contraction, boundaried replacement, the HSS-forced induced-P13 CT1 stage, the routed maximum-packing CT12 remainder stage, the exhaustive CT10 P13 attachment-label algebra, the ordered CT6 degree-surplus ledger with per-slot activation, the CT15 baseline and free/blocked pair-response stages, the CT9 free-anchor and capacity-token ledgers, the exact 25-role coupled overload decision, and the Type B development through its graph-derived high-center choice/overlap trichotomy.
workflows.purpose: Inspect the exact Mersenne target algebra, lexicographic minimal selection, CT2 criticality and bridge contraction, boundaried replacement, the HSS-forced induced-P13 CT1 stage, the routed maximum-packing CT12 remainder stage, the exhaustive CT10 P13 attachment-label algebra, the ordered CT6 degree-surplus ledger with per-slot activation, the CT15 baseline and free/blocked pair-response stages, the CT9 free-anchor and capacity-token ledgers, the exact 25-role coupled overload decision, and the Type B development through its graph-derived high-center choice/overlap trichotomy.

stages.title: Official Erdős 64 statement
stages.summary: The pinned public boundary asks for a power-of-two cycle in every finite graph of minimum degree at least three.

stages.title: Executable internal problem
stages.summary: The same Mathlib graph is paired with an explicit finite inspection order and an executable but equivalent length predicate.

stages.title: CT1 Mersenne target algebra
stages.summary: CT1 validates one rooted return or constructs the exact avoiding residual from disjoint return sets.

stages.title: CT2 no proper core
stages.summary: A supplied proper finite subgraph is one certified local reduction; target transport and lexicographic minimality force its minimum degree to be at most two.

stages.title: Routed CT2 deletion criticality
stages.summary: The certified CT1 residual enters local CT2 discovery; its exact disabled result forces every edge to have a degree-three endpoint.

stages.title: CT3 boundaried replacement
stages.summary: Literal packed-graph CT3 gluing derives whole-rank decrease, minimum-degree preservation, and target transport from local replacement data.

stages.title: CT1 induced-P₁₃ branch
stages.summary: Node [15] makes the exhaustive P₁₃-free decision on the literal node-[14] stage; node [16] closes exactly its yes constructor with HSS and retains the literal no constructor.

stages.title: CT12 induced-P₁₃ packing
stages.summary: Node [17] selects a deterministic maximal vertex-disjoint induced-P₁₃ family and retains CT12 disjointness, saturation, terminal, trace, totality, nonemptiness, and local work evidence.

stages.title: CT10 P₁₃ attachment-label algebra
stages.summary: One CT10 stage classifies every nonempty attachment label on the fixed thirteen-position path, certifies the exact 399-row table and size distribution, and defines the manuscript compatibility and two-step curvature relations.

stages.title: Exact quadratic surplus-scale split
stages.summary: The actual degree surplus is compared with the squared fixed homogeneous-cap scale by one natural-number comparison, giving the exhaustive node-[19] non-near-cubic or bounded branch.

stages.title: Node [25] exact Residual A
stages.summary: The literal node-[24] active cursor is mapped to the canonical CT12 packing complement, its exact partition and scaled largeness, and hereditary induced-P13-freeness.

stages.title: Node [26] Residual A panel continuation
stages.summary: The framework maps the literal node-[25] output and records only that the Part-II remainder name is the same canonical packing complement.

stages.title: Node [27] no internal three-core
stages.summary: On the exact node-[26] remainder, the existing graph lemmas exclude both an induced internal minimum-degree-three support and the manuscript's stronger finite internal subgraph form.

stages.title: Exact P13-remainder positive deficiency
stages.summary: Node [28] maps the literal node-[27] cursor and records exactly the graph-owned positive-deficiency sum on that unchanged remainder.

stages.title: Exact external-incidence supply
stages.summary: The literal node-[28] remainder deficiency injects into its R--W boundary incidences, those incidences embed in the selected-window token schedule, and the exact schedule identity gives 15p13+sigma_W together with its surplus-adjusted form.

stages.title: Exact remainder wedge lower bound
stages.summary: The graph-owned degree-count identity is applied to every supplied finite component support and to the exact remainder. Node [29]'s incidence supply gives the finite window-only error, while the reusable real transport handles both manuscript rates without scanning another universe.

stages.title: Exact curvature target-rank definition
stages.summary: The literal raw remainder wedges and their cardinality are verified. The public node value is the attained maximum over the graph-owned carrier/proposal/realization/admissibility/functionality universe, with exact W2(R) upper bound and no enumeration of quotient or realization families.

stages.title: Exact curvature rank-loss dichotomy
stages.summary: The exact node-[31] maximum is compared with the complete raw wedge cardinality. The two original edges are exhaustive: strict rank loss, or equality with the full schedule. This proof-level comparison evaluates no family of subfamilies, quotients, or contexts.

stages.title: Node [33] rank-reducing branch marker
stages.summary: Node [33] names only node [32]'s literal strict-rank-loss constructor as a PUnit branch marker. The decision proof remains at node [32], and circuit extraction belongs to node [35].

stages.title: Exact full-curvature-rank residual
stages.summary: Node [34] consumes node [32]'s literal full-rank constructor as a branch marker. Core retains that constructor and the complete accumulated ledger without manufacturing a Boolean-capacity or table-product consequence.

stages.title: Node [21] multi-scale curvature interface
stages.summary: Node [21] fixes the exact 91-barrier output on node [19]'s literal no continuation. Its cached finite certificates prove the semantic table, safe/flat counts, strict 118-bit rate, and polynomial work bound; Core appends that verified output to the accumulated ledger.

stages.title: Nodes [22]--[24] exact density branch
stages.summary: From the accumulated node-[21] ledger, node [22] makes the paper's exact scalar dichotomy, node [23] records the strict yes-edge overflow without an auxiliary assumption, and node [24] retains the complementary window cap, its reusable high-entropy transformer, and the accumulated hot/cold split.

stages.title: Node [35] functional rank circuit
stages.summary: On node [33]'s strict-loss marker, CT15 extracts the canonical proof-carrying pair circuit. Its candidate supplies the exact support-stratified determination certificate, while Core preserves the accumulated residual and sibling branches.

stages.summary: Node [36] consumes node [35]'s exact certificate and audits only the original atom interface. It returns either one concrete atom-context mismatch or universal equality at that atom; carrier-level target completeness is not transported across interfaces.

stages.title: Target-defective quotient terminal

stages.title: Original-versus-enlarged carrier decision
stages.summary: On node [36]'s universal edge, node [38] compares the certificate's final carrier with its original atom. The framework returns exactly the at-original or strict-enlargement constructor and retains the carrier-indexed representative without searching a support universe.

stages.title: Proper enlarged-carrier CT3 terminal

stages.title: One--three repair identity

stages.title: Whole-carrier exact-label barrier

stages.title: Whole-carrier rank-drop contradiction
stages.summary: Node [46] applies node [45]'s injectivity to the distinct coordinate pair identified by node [35], closing the whole-carrier rank-drop edge.

stages.title: Exact node-[47] full-rank handoff

stages.title: Forced curvature cost
stages.summary: Node [48] fixes the paper's exact curvature-cost constants on the literal node-[47] leaf. The inherited wedge supply, node-[34] full-rank inequality, and node-[24] high-entropy implication prove both finite accounting bounds; Core then transports them to the real-valued cost conclusions.

stages.title: Realized remainder-state entropy
stages.summary: Node [49] projects the current residual's compatible global completions to the fixed remainder carried by node [31]. Its state count and normalized entropy therefore range over exactly the realized remainder states, without creating an ambient graph family or enumerating a product universe.

stages.title: Exact manuscript entropy dichotomy
stages.summary: Node [50] retains node [49]'s exact realized-state count and applies the Core-owned ordered split to the equivalent natural-power comparison n^|R| <= |G_real(R)|^10. It produces exactly the original high and strict-low edges with zero primitive inspections.

stages.title: High-entropy remainder bit contribution
stages.summary: Node [51] consumes only node [50]'s exact high power inequality and takes base-two logarithms symbolically, deriving (|R|/10) log₂ n ≤ log₂ |G_real(R)| without enumerating the realized state type.

stages.title: Node [52] joint window--remainder accounting
stages.summary: On node [51]'s literal high leaf, node [52] instantiates Core's symbolic base-plus-dependent-choice capacity theorem and powered joint-normalization profile. Remainder-preserving reglue fits the realized remainder family and every retained hot-window choice in one skeleton code; the powered inequality retains discarded hot scales and cold-window mass explicitly, without enumerating a product.

stages.title: Accumulated remaining-budget decision
stages.summary: Node [53] consumes the exact low constructor retained beside node [52], compares the literal remaining labelled-skeleton bits with the inherited full-rank curvature cost, and retains both original outgoing edges. The high branch and every earlier bypass remain unchanged.

stages.title: Guarded entropy-cap closure
stages.summary: Node [54] terminalizes the high node-[52] leaf. On node [53]'s small leaf it decides the exact product-fit inequality: fit closes by Core's powered-budget transfer, while failure remains a typed degradation residual. The original large-budget leaf remains independently live.

interfaceBindings.summary: A rooted return is the positive code and exact return-set disjointness supplies the avoiding execution.
The application supplies one finite proper-subgraph certificate; the framework proves rank decrease, runs the canonical deletion-C2 path, and exposes a constant work bound.

A local representative supplies boundary response, internal degree, and local smallness; literal gluing derives whole-rank decrease, baseline preservation, and target transport before the minimality contradiction.

The problem supplies a compact thirteen-bit legality predicate proved equivalent to graph-theoretic cycle avoidance; the generic CT10 profile classifies the 8192 local candidates, audits the complete 399-row table, and retains a quadratic work certificate.

from itertools import combinations
import numpy as np, math

L=[]
for mask in range(1,1<<13):
    inds=[i for i in range(13) if (mask>>i)&1]
    ok=True
    for a,b in combinations(inds,2):
        if abs(a-b) in (2,6):
            ok=False
            break
    if ok:
        L.append(mask)
assert len(L)==399

def indices(mask):
    return [i for i in range(13) if (mask>>i)&1]

Ms=[]
for s in range(15):
    forb={2**r-s-2 for r in range(2,20) if 0 <= 2**r-s-2 <= 12}
    M=np.ones((len(L),len(L)), dtype=np.int8)
    for i,S in enumerate(L):
        Si=indices(S)
        for j,T in enumerate(L):
            Tj=indices(T)
            bad=any(abs(a-b) in forb for a in Si for b in Tj)
            if bad:
                M[i,j]=0
    Ms.append(M)

total=0.0
vals=[]
for a in range(1,15):
    for b in range(1,15-a): # a,b >= 1 and a+b <= 14
        middle_counts=Ms[a].astype(np.int32) @ Ms[b].astype(np.int32)
        W=int(middle_counts.sum())
        bad=int((middle_counts*(1-Ms[a+b])).sum())
        flat=W-bad
        val=math.log2(W/flat)
        vals.append((a,b,W,bad,flat,val))
        total += val

print(len(vals))
print(total)
print(min(vals, key=lambda x: x[-1]))

from fractions import Fraction
from itertools import combinations

labels=[]
for mask in range(1,1<<13):
    S=tuple(i for i in range(13) if (mask>>i)&1)
    if all(abs(a-b) not in (2,6) for a,b in combinations(S,2)):
        labels.append(S)

powers={2**k for k in range(2,20)}

def C(s,S,T):
    return all(s+2+abs(i-j) not in powers for i in S for j in T)

n=len(labels)
adj=[0]*n
edges=0
for i,S in enumerate(labels):
    for j in range(i+1,n):
        if C(2,S,labels[j]):
            adj[i] |= 1<<j
            adj[j] |= 1<<i
            edges += 1

def maximum_clique(mask):
    best_size=0
    best_bits=0
    def color_sort(P):
        verts=[]
        colors=[]
        U=P
        color=0
        while U:
            color += 1
            Q=U
            while Q:
                bit=Q & -Q
                v=bit.bit_length()-1
                verts.append(v)
                colors.append(color)
                U &= ~bit
                Q &= ~bit
                Q &= ~adj[v]
        return verts, colors
    def expand(size,bits,P):
        nonlocal best_size,best_bits
        if not P:
            if size > best_size:
                best_size=size
                best_bits=bits
            return
        verts,colors=color_sort(P)
        for idx in range(len(verts)-1,-1,-1):
            if size + colors[idx] <= best_size:
                return
            v=verts[idx]
            bit=1<<v
            if P & bit:
                expand(size+1,bits|bit,P & adj[v])
                P &= ~bit
                if size + P.bit_count() <= best_size:
                    return
    expand(0,0,mask)
    return best_size,best_bits

omega,wit=maximum_clique((1<<n)-1)
omega_ns=0
wit_ns=0
for i,S in enumerate(labels):
    if len(S) >= 2:
        size,bits=maximum_clique(adj[i] | (1<<i))
        if size > omega_ns:
            omega_ns=size
            wit_ns=bits

def bit_labels(bits):
    return [labels[i] for i in range(n) if (bits>>i)&1]

simple_charge_k_le_7=min(
    (Fraction(3-k,1)-Fraction(1,4)-Fraction(1,4)
     + Fraction(3,4)*(k-1), k)
    for k in range(4,8)
)
charge_k8_no_closed=Fraction(3-8,1)-Fraction(1,4)+8*Fraction(3,4)

print('labels', n)
print('fan_safe_edges', edges)
print('omega_C2', omega)
print('omega_C2_witness', bit_labels(wit))
print('omega_with_nonsingleton', omega_ns)
print('nonsingleton_witness', bit_labels(wit_ns))
print('simple_charge_k_le_7', simple_charge_k_le_7[0], 'at_k',
      simple_charge_k_le_7[1])
print('charge_k8_no_closed', charge_k8_no_closed)

proofSteps.title: Official theorem boundary
proofSteps.plainExplanation: The public Lean proposition uses the same finite simple graph and asks for exactly the power-of-two cycle asserted by the manuscript's main theorem.

declarationGroups.title: Public mathematical statement
declarationGroups.explanation: This declaration is the exact Mathlib-facing proposition that the completed formalization must prove.

proofSteps.scopeNotes: The public statement is exact, but the current verified workflow is explicitly a partial prefix and does not yet prove this proposition.
proofSteps.workBound: Statement boundary only; no computation is performed.

proofSteps.title: Faithful executable target
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Lean equips the same Mathlib graph with finite inspection data and replaces the unbounded exponent quantifier by a bounded decidable search, then proves that this executable target is equivalent to the official conclusion.

declarationGroups.title: Encoding and equivalence bridge
declarationGroups.explanation: The static input supplies deterministic finite data; the two equivalence theorems prove that neither the cycle predicate nor its exponent range has changed.

proofSteps.scopeNotes: Finite enumeration and adjacency decisions control execution order only; they do not introduce a second graph representation or weaken the theorem.
proofSteps.workBound: The exponent search is bounded by the candidate cycle length.

proofSteps.title: Mersenne return target algebra
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Removing one edge from a power-of-two cycle leaves a return path whose length is one less than a power of two. Conversely, adjoining the root edge to such a return produces the target cycle. CT1 records either a supplied return or universal avoidance.
proofSteps.formalStatement: G \text{ has a } C_{2^j} \iff \exists e,\ 2^j-1 \in R_e(G)

declarationGroups.title: Exact graph-theoretic equivalences
declarationGroups.explanation: These theorems identify the internal target with existence of a Mersenne return and identify target avoidance with disjointness of every return set from the Mersenne lengths.

declarationGroups.title: Positive and avoiding CT1 executions
declarationGroups.explanation: Node [6] stores the exact certificate decision. Node [7] interprets its C1 certificate as the official target, closes that terminal against inherited counterexample avoidance, and retains only the literal avoiding run. The K₄ run independently pins the positive path.

declarationGroups.title: Problem-to-framework encoding
declarationGroups.explanation: The Erdős encoding instantiates the reusable edge-rooted CT1 certificate profile without asking the application to manufacture a CT outcome.

proofSteps.scopeNotes: This covers nodes [5]--[7]. The C1 edge is the manuscript target terminal; the exact avoiding edge is the sole successor consumed by node [8].
proofSteps.workBound: One certificate check for C1 and zero checks for the proof-carrying avoiding execution.

proofSteps.title: No proper minimum-degree-three core
proofSteps.plainExplanation: If a proper subgraph still had minimum degree at least three, target avoidance would be inherited and it would be a lexicographically smaller counterexample. Certified CT2 reduction formalizes this contradiction for one arbitrary supplied proper subgraph.

declarationGroups.title: Mathematical conclusion
declarationGroups.explanation: This theorem is the direct Lean form of the manuscript lemma: every proper finite subgraph of the selected counterexample has minimum degree at most two.

declarationGroups.title: Certified CT2 execution audit
declarationGroups.explanation: These declarations prove the exact deletion-C2 terminal, typed trace, totality, one-check count, and constant polynomial budget for the supplied proper-subgraph certificate.

declarationGroups.title: Composed prefix and predecessor
declarationGroups.explanation: The node consumes the sole avoiding successor left by node [7]. Framework dependent-successor storage retains the exact CT1 run and selected context; no root-level prefix is reconstructed.

declarationGroups.title: Certified-reduction interface
declarationGroups.explanation: The packed boundaried graph data supplies the concrete smaller object, while the reusable graph profile owns rank decrease, cycle transport, and the canonical CT2 run.

proofSteps.scopeNotes: The framework checks one proof-specified proper subgraph at a time; the theorem universally quantifies over certificates and does not enumerate subgraphs.
proofSteps.workBound: Exactly one local certificate check; degree-zero polynomial budget.

proofSteps.title: Edge-deletion criticality
proofSteps.plainExplanation: An edge with both endpoints of degree at least four could be deleted while preserving minimum degree three, and deletion cannot create a new cycle. Minimality therefore forces every edge to touch a degree-three vertex.
proofSteps.formalStatement: xy \in E(G) \Longrightarrow d_G(x)=3 \lor d_G(y)=3;\qquad V_{\ge 4}(G) \text{ is independent}

declarationGroups.title: Node [9] deletion-criticality successor
declarationGroups.explanation: The literal node-[8] successor is consumed once. The graph-owned local deletion theorem supplies exactly the degree-three endpoint alternative, which node [9] appends to the accumulated ledger.

proofSteps.formalStatement: n^{|R|}\le |\mathcal G_{\rm real}(R)|^{10}\Longrightarrow (|R|/10)\log_2 n\le\log_2|\mathcal G_{\rm real}(R)|

declarationGroups.title: Exact node-50 high-entropy transfer
declarationGroups.explanation: The framework retains the literal node-[50] decision and its high-edge proof in the accumulated ledger. The Erdős layer adds only the manuscript bit inequality, using node-[49]'s registered entropy identity.

proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [51] is only the high-branch arithmetic handoff. It feeds the original node-[52] window-plus-remainder accounting step; it does not prove window/remainder independence or the downstream entropy cap.
proofSteps.workBound: No new finite scan and no real-number search. The proof reuses the zero-check node-[50] ordered split and node-[49]'s symbolic entropy identity; it evaluates no graph family, state universe, context universe, assignment cube, or Boolean universe.

proofSteps.title: Accumulated node-[52] framework handoff
proofSteps.plainExplanation: The framework maps only node [51]'s exact high continuation and retains every sibling unchanged. Node [52] proves its same-skeleton joint-capacity and normalized budget locally on that literal leaf.

declarationGroups.title: Branch-preserving local feasibility handoff
declarationGroups.explanation: Core owns exact predecessor retention and low-edge pass-through. The Erdős layer proves the paper's same-context joint capacity, powered normalization, logarithmic transport, and final joint budget on the incoming high leaf.

proofSteps.scopeNotes: The framework transition and every same-context capacity and normalization theorem listed above are kernel-checked on the literal node-[51] residual. Node [52] creates no decision or additional branch.
proofSteps.workBound: Zero semantic checks and no graph, context, state-product, assignment-cube, or Boolean-universe enumeration.

proofSteps.title: Window-plus-remainder accounting
proofSteps.plainExplanation: The remaining paper-facing density consequence must rewrite node [52]'s exact joint budget into the displayed scalar normalization and solve that scalar inequality. The same-context finite and logarithmic accounting that supplies the premise is already kernel-checked.
proofSteps.scopeNotes: Framework routing and the exact finite, powered, logarithmic, and joint-budget propositions are implemented. What remains here is only the manuscript's final scalar normalization to the displayed theta bound.
proofSteps.workBound: Pure symbolic arithmetic over inherited ledger facts, with zero ambient graph, context, state-product, assignment-cube, or Boolean-universe enumeration.

proofSteps.title: Node-[54] guarded entropy-cap closure
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [54] closes the high edge directly. On the low-small edge Core decides the exact product-fit guard; a proof closes by powered-budget transfer, while its negation is retained as a typed residual and joins the curvature-independent node-[56] continuation.

manuscriptRefs.label: prop:entropy-high-theta
manuscriptRefs.title: Entropy cap closes the high-theta branch

declarationGroups.title: Verified guarded Part-IV closure
declarationGroups.explanation: The high edge is a direct symbolic order contradiction. On the low-small edge the framework decides product fit, closes only the positive guard, and retains its exact negation beside the original large branch.

proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [54] closes the [52] edge and the product-fit subcase of [53] small. Product-fit failure and [53] large remain explicit live residuals; the separate repair overlay records their common node-[56] join without changing the original numbered diagram.
proofSteps.workBound: Pure symbolic arithmetic over inherited ledger facts. No semantic checks, graph families, context families, state-product scans, assignment cubes, or Boolean universes are enumerated.

proofSteps.title: Accumulated node-[53] budget decision
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [53] consumes only node [50]'s exact low constructor. It compares the exact forced curvature power with the flat curvature power times the inherited low-entropy allowance, retaining both manuscript edges. The high constructor and node [52] output pass through unchanged.

declarationGroups.title: Exact branch-preserving remaining-budget split
declarationGroups.explanation: Core acts on the literal node-[50] low constructor retained beside node [52], preserves the high continuation, and executes the complementary natural-power comparison only there. The Erdős layer supplies the three exact powers and no predecessor plumbing.

proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [53] performs only the diagrammed dichotomy. Its decision is kernel-checked; node [54] and node [55] consume its two constructors separately.
proofSteps.workBound: One proof-level natural-order comparison; no graph family, state family, product, context, assignment, or Boolean universe is evaluated.

proofSteps.title: Accumulated node-[55] large-budget residual
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [55] appends Residual C only to node [53]'s large leaf. Core simultaneously preserves the independent product-fit failure residual created at node [54].

declarationGroups.title: Framework-owned Residual-C handoff
declarationGroups.explanation: Core continues only the original large-budget leaf and leaves the product-fit failure untouched. The Erdős payload contains only node [55]'s local data.

proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [55] only names the surviving large-budget residual and carries its existing local branch facts forward.
proofSteps.workBound: Zero checks; only accumulated branch transport.

proofSteps.title: Accumulated node-[56] large-budget net cap
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [56] applies the same curvature-independent net-cap producer to both live reasons: failed product fit and the original node-[53] large-budget leaf. Core hides the reason from the producer and preserves it as provenance.

declarationGroups.title: Finite net cap and tau arithmetic
declarationGroups.explanation: The finite cap comes from node [30]'s producer inside the carried node-[31] payload. Node [56] performs only symbolic normalization and the exact rational comparison tau_win < 1/4.

proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [56] supplies the same finite, error-bearing net-deficiency cap on both live residual reasons. The later negative-support localization is Part V.
proofSteps.workBound: Zero graph checks and constant-size rational arithmetic; no graph, state, context, product, assignment, or Boolean universe is enumerated.

proofSteps.title: Node-[57] large-budget net-cap handoff
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [57] consumes the exact node-[56] merged residual and carries forward the finite net-deficiency cap and tau_win < 1/4 on each live leaf.

declarationGroups.title: Framework-owned node-[57] handoff
declarationGroups.explanation: The node uses only Core's dependent stage map over the literal node-[56] predecessor. The payload contains no new state or family.

proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [57] performs only the Part-V net-cap handoff; node [58] defines the net charge and node [59] performs the sign split.
proofSteps.workBound: Zero checks; only accumulated branch transport.

proofSteps.title: Node-[58] net-charge definition
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [58] consumes the exact node-[57] successor and names the quarter-scaled integer net charge on the identical remainder.

declarationGroups.title: Exact quarter-scaled net-charge numerator
declarationGroups.explanation: The node introduces only the paper's net-charge notation in finite integer form. It does not perform node [59]'s sign dichotomy.

proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [58] defines the local net-charge object and routes unchanged to node [59].
proofSteps.workBound: Zero checks; no graph, support, state, context, or family enumeration.

proofSteps.title: Node-[59] net-charge sign split
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [59] consumes the exact node-[58] successor and asks precisely whether the quarter-scaled net-charge numerator is nonnegative.

declarationGroups.title: Framework-owned sign dichotomy
declarationGroups.explanation: The Erdős layer defines only the yes/no sign predicates. Core executes the dependent decision over the literal node-[58] stage and preserves both branch constructors.

proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [59] performs only the sign decision. Node [60] closes the yes branch; node [61] consumes the no branch.
proofSteps.workBound: Zero checks; no graph, support, state, context, family, or Boolean-universe enumeration.

proofSteps.title: Node-[60] net-cap contradiction
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [60] closes the nonnegative node-[59] branch by using node [58]'s exact net-charge name and node [57]'s strict negative quarter-scaled cap.

declarationGroups.title: Framework-owned yes-branch closure
declarationGroups.explanation: Core eliminates only the node-[59] yes constructor. The Erdős proof supplies the local integer contradiction, and Core retains the literal negative constructor for node [61].

proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [60] is terminal only for the nonnegative net-charge branch; it does not localize the negative support.
proofSteps.workBound: Zero checks; no graph, support, state, context, family, or Boolean-universe enumeration.

proofSteps.title: Hot/cold window interface
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [145] consumes the exact node-[24] low residual and advances the cursor to the accumulated sequential hot/cold window interface without copying ledger data into an Erdős-owned output record.

declarationGroups.title: Accumulated hot/cold ledger exposure
declarationGroups.explanation: The framework maps the literal node-[24] residual to node [145]; the Erdős layer only exposes the framework-owned hot/cold cursor.

proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [145] creates no new branch; it names the single accumulated hot/cold interface consumed by the cold-window continuation.
proofSteps.workBound: Zero new executable checks and no graph, state, context, or Boolean universe enumeration.

proofSteps.title: Route-8 density threshold
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [146] performs the exact threshold decision 78 p13 < n on the node-[145] active residual and exposes the yes/no arithmetic payloads.

declarationGroups.title: Framework-owned focused threshold decision
declarationGroups.explanation: Core performs the branch decision on the literal node-[145] residual; the node proves theta and tau arithmetic for the yes edge and the cross-multiplied lower threshold for the no edge.

proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [146] only decides the route-8 threshold and routes the two original edges.
proofSteps.workBound: One primitive arithmetic comparison; no finite graph scan.

proofSteps.title: Route-8 private-carrier arithmetic
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [147] consumes node [146]'s yes edge and proves the coefficient gap and positive margin needed by the route-8 private-carrier residual.

declarationGroups.title: Route-8 arithmetic residual
declarationGroups.explanation: The checked payload derives both rational inequalities directly from the node-[146] yes constructor, using only framework branch transport.

proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [147] records the checked arithmetic prefix of the route-8 residual; it does not assert an unimplemented carrier collision.
proofSteps.workBound: Zero graph checks; rational arithmetic only.

proofSteps.title: Live-hot entropy-cap decision
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [148] consumes node [146]'s no edge, defines the paper hot/cold demand quantities, proves the exact demand partition, and lets Core decide the live-hot cap.

declarationGroups.title: Exact live-hot cap split
declarationGroups.explanation: Core focuses node [146]'s no continuation and performs the cap decision. The Erdős layer supplies only the paper constants and the hot/cold demand partition.

proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [148] has exactly the two original outgoing edges: cap success to [149] and cap failure to [150].
proofSteps.workBound: One primitive arithmetic comparison; no graph, context, state-family, or Boolean-universe scan.

proofSteps.title: Live-hot density-cap terminal
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [149] consumes only the cap-success edge of node [148] and records the terminal live-hot density-cap payload.

declarationGroups.title: Density-cap terminal payload
declarationGroups.explanation: The framework maps the exact node-[148] yes continuation to the terminal live-hot cap cursor without copying branch facts into an Erdős-owned output record.

proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [149] is terminal for the live-hot cap-success edge only.
proofSteps.workBound: Zero local checks.

proofSteps.title: Cold residual after hot failure
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [150] consumes node [148]'s cap-failure edge, exposes the exact cold-window count, and carries the cold branch as a framework-owned no-continuation cursor.
proofSteps.formalStatement: \neg(D_{\rm total}\le A)\Longrightarrow C_{\rm cold}\ \text{with residual requirements}

declarationGroups.title: Exact cold residual and requirements
declarationGroups.explanation: Node [150] uses the framework no-continuation from node [148] and keeps downstream facts in the carried residual rather than an Erdős-owned output record.

proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [150] produces the cold residual; it does not close the later cold branch.
proofSteps.workBound: Zero local checks and no ambient universe enumeration.

proofSteps.title: Ambient-cubic cold handoff
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [151] partitions the literal cold-window list into ambient-cubic and non-cubic windows and bounds the non-cubic loss by the inherited near-cubic surplus budget.
proofSteps.formalStatement: C_{\rm cubic}+C_{\rm noncubic}=C_{\rm cold},\qquad C_{\rm noncubic}^{2}\le B_{\rm surplus}

declarationGroups.title: Cubic/non-cubic cold partition
declarationGroups.explanation: The node works only with the cold-window list carried by node [150]; it proves the partition, duplicate-free cubic list, non-cubic surplus bound, and square-loss bound.

proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [151] is the local ambient-cubic handoff for cold windows; later bounded-germ work starts after this handoff.
proofSteps.workBound: Symbolic list partition over the carried cold list; no graph-family enumeration.

proofSteps.title: Cold-window branch-excess schedule
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [152] creates the exact branch-excess schedule with thirteen selected stubs per ambient-cubic cold window and proves the branch-excess count inequality.

declarationGroups.title: Thirteen-stub branch-excess schedule
declarationGroups.explanation: The schedule is built from the exact node-[151] cubic cold-window list. Its length, nodup proof, same-window projection, and cold-count inequality are kernel checked.

proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [152] only supplies the branch-excess schedule and exact inequality consumed by node [153].
proofSteps.workBound: Zero semantic graph scans; fixed thirteen-stub schedule per carried cubic cold window.

proofSteps.title: Cold-corridor first-failure scan
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [153] runs the graph-owned first-failure corridor machine on each node-[152] scheduled stub, proves the scan count, and carries the exact residual requirements to node [154].

declarationGroups.title: Graph-owned first-failure scan
declarationGroups.explanation: The graph layer supplies the corridor producer and totality theorem. The Erdős node schedules the carried branch-excess stubs, records hit/germ results, and exposes only the residual requirements prescribed for node [154].

proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [153] is green because its local scan and residual handoff are implemented. The residual requirements it outputs are node [154]'s frontier, not unfinished node-[153] work.
proofSteps.workBound: One graph-owned first-failure run per scheduled branch-excess stub; no graph, context, state-family, assignment, or Boolean universe is enumerated.

proofSteps.title: Cold G1 target split
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [154] consumes the exact node-[153] first-failure residual and uses the framework focused-branch decision to split on whether a scheduled cold corridor has an F1 dyadic target hit.

declarationGroups.title: Framework G1 split
declarationGroups.explanation: The node defines G1 by the graph-owned F1 predicate on the carried branch-excess schedule, executes the framework focused decision, and continues the no branch as an exact no-F1 residual for the next G2/G3 split.

proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [154] proves only the G1/no-G1 split. The no branch deliberately remains a no-F1 residual for nodes [156] and [157].
proofSteps.workBound: One focused framework branch decision; no application-owned route or replacement state.

proofSteps.title: Cold G1 dyadic-cycle terminal
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [155] closes exactly the G1 yes leaf by turning the framework F1 hit into the graph-owned power-of-two cycle target and contradicting the inherited target-avoidance theorem.

declarationGroups.title: Framework G1 closure
declarationGroups.explanation: The node consumes only the G1 yes leaf, obtains the target cycle with the graph-owned F1-to-target conversion, records the existing CT1 run certificate, and closes the branch through the framework focused-branch terminal combinator.

proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [155] closes only the G1 terminal edge. The no-G1 cold residual remains with node [154]'s no continuation for later G2/G3 routing.
proofSteps.workBound: Zero enumeration beyond the predecessor's carried corridor scan.

proofSteps.title: Cold G2/G3 split
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [156] consumes the exact no-G1 residual from node [154] and uses the framework focused-branch decision to split the same scheduled corridor stages into an F4 G2 event branch and a no-F4 G3 residual.

declarationGroups.title: Framework G2/G3 split
declarationGroups.explanation: The node defines G2 by the graph-owned F4 predicate on the predecessor's scheduled corridor stage lists. The framework decides that focused branch and continues the no branch as the exact G3 residual for node [157].

proofSteps.scopeNotes: This is the verified G2/G3 split below no-G1. It does not yet close the G2 event branch, so node [156] remains outside the whole-node green list.
proofSteps.workBound: One framework focused decision over the already carried corridor scan; no application-owned route or new state object.

declarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.node153EveryScheduledFirstFailure_total
declarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} {residual : Internal.InitialResidual V} (active : Internal.Node148Active V residual),
  ∀ entry ∈ Internal.node152BranchExcessSchedule active,
    (∃ hit data,
        StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdCorridor.runFirstFailure ⋯ Internal.PowerOfTwoLength
            Internal.powerOfTwoLengthDecidable entry =
          StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteFirstFailure.Profile.Result.first hit data) ∨
      ∃ (noEvent :
        ∀
          stage ∈
            ((StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdCorridor.firstFailureProfile ⋯ Internal.PowerOfTwoLength
                    Internal.powerOfTwoLengthDecidable).stages
                entry).values,
          ¬(StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdCorridor.firstFailureProfile ⋯ Internal.PowerOfTwoLength
                  Internal.powerOfTwoLengthDecidable).Event
              entry stage),
        ∃ germ,
          StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdCorridor.runFirstFailure ⋯ Internal.PowerOfTwoLength
              Internal.powerOfTwoLengthDecidable entry =
            StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteFirstFailure.Profile.Result.germ noEvent germ

declarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.node17Total
declarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} {residual : Internal.InitialResidual V} (stage : Internal.Node17Stage residual),
  ∃ result,
    StructuralExhaustion.CT12.Outcome.Valid
        (StructuralExhaustion.CT12.ListPeeling.capability Internal.packedStaticInput.problem
          (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathPacking.Window (Internal.Node17Context stage.previous).G.object 13))
        result.outcome ∧
      StructuralExhaustion.CT12.Graph.ValidTrace
          (StructuralExhaustion.CT12.ListPeeling.capability Internal.packedStaticInput.problem
            (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathPacking.Window (Internal.Node17Context stage.previous).G.object 13))
          (StructuralExhaustion.CT12.ExecutionResult.trace
            (StructuralExhaustion.CT12.ListPeeling.capability Internal.packedStaticInput.problem
              (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathPacking.Window (Internal.Node17Context stage.previous).G.object
                13))
            result) ∧
        result.iterations ≤ FinEnum.card (Internal.Node17Context stage.previous).G.Vertex ∧
          (StructuralExhaustion.CT12.ExecutionResult.trace
                (StructuralExhaustion.CT12.ListPeeling.capability Internal.packedStaticInput.problem
                  (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathPacking.Window (Internal.Node17Context stage.previous).G.object
                    13))
                result).length ≤
            4 * FinEnum.card (Internal.Node17Context stage.previous).G.Vertex + 3

declarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.runP13LabelCT10_terminal
declarations.type:

  (Internal.runP13LabelCT10 ctx).terminal = StructuralExhaustion.CT10.Graph.Terminal.exhaustive

declarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.runP13LabelCT10_total
declarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget),
  ∃ result,
    result.terminal = StructuralExhaustion.CT10.Graph.Terminal.exhaustive ∧
      StructuralExhaustion.CT10.ExecutionResult.trace
            (Internal.p13LabelClassification.capability Internal.PackedProblem) (Internal.p13LabelCT10Input ctx)
            result =
          [StructuralExhaustion.CT10.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT10.Graph.NodeId.table,
            StructuralExhaustion.CT10.Graph.NodeId.direct, StructuralExhaustion.CT10.Graph.NodeId.missing,
            StructuralExhaustion.CT10.Graph.NodeId.exhaustiveTerminal] ∧
        StructuralExhaustion.CT10.Outcome.Valid (Internal.p13LabelClassification.capability Internal.PackedProblem)
            (Internal.p13LabelCT10Input ctx) result.outcome ∧
          StructuralExhaustion.CT10.Graph.ValidTrace (Internal.p13LabelClassification.capability Internal.PackedProblem)
            (Internal.p13LabelCT10Input ctx)
            (StructuralExhaustion.CT10.ExecutionResult.trace
              (Internal.p13LabelClassification.capability Internal.PackedProblem) (Internal.p13LabelCT10Input ctx)
              result)

      (Internal.p13LabelClassification.input ctx.toBranchContext) (Internal.runP13LabelCT10 ctx) =

declarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.DensityAsymptoticTransport.nat_partition_complement_lower
declarations.type:
∀ {rate complementRate width budget mass remainder order : ℕ},
  rate = complementRate + width * budget →
    remainder + width * mass = order → rate * mass ≤ budget * order → complementRate * order ≤ rate * remainder

declarations.type:
∀ {forced flat stateCount upper scale : ℕ},
  forced ≤ flat * stateCount → stateCount ^ scale < upper → 0 < flat → forced ^ scale < flat ^ scale * upper

declarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.IsCounterexample
declarations.type: (P : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem) → (P.Ambient → Prop) → P.Ambient → Prop

declarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.OneThreeRepair.identity
declarations.type:
∀ (internalVertices boundaryLeaves internalEdges cycleRank surplus : ℤ),
  3 * internalVertices + surplus + boundaryLeaves = 2 * (internalEdges + boundaryLeaves) →
    cycleRank = internalEdges - internalVertices + 1 → internalVertices = boundaryLeaves - 2 + 2 * cycleRank - surplus

declarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.PoweredJointNormalization.Profile.logb_withError
declarations.type:
∀ (profile : StructuralExhaustion.Core.PoweredJointNormalization.Profile),
  0 < profile.stateCount →
    0 < profile.base →
      0 < profile.capacity →
        ↑profile.exponent * Real.logb 2 ↑profile.stateCount + ↑profile.desiredExponent * Real.logb 2 ↑profile.base ≤
          ↑profile.exponent * Real.logb 2 ↑profile.capacity + ↑profile.errorExponent * Real.logb 2 ↑profile.base

  profile.stateCount ^ profile.exponent * profile.base ^ profile.desiredExponent ≤

declarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdCorridor.runFirstFailure_total
declarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} {object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V}
  (producer : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdCorridor.Producer object) (LengthOK : ℕ → Prop)
  (lengthOKDecidable : DecidablePred LengthOK)
  (stub : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdCorridor.CubicStub object),
  (∃ hit data,
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdCorridor.runFirstFailure producer LengthOK lengthOKDecidable stub =
        StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteFirstFailure.Profile.Result.first hit data) ∨
    ∃ (noEvent :
      ∀
        stage ∈
          ((StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdCorridor.firstFailureProfile producer LengthOK
                  lengthOKDecidable).stages
              stub).values,
        ¬(StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdCorridor.firstFailureProfile producer LengthOK
                lengthOKDecidable).Event
            stub stage),
      ∃ germ,
        StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathColdCorridor.runFirstFailure producer LengthOK lengthOKDecidable stub =
          StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteFirstFailure.Profile.Result.germ noEvent germ

declarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.remainderPositiveDeficiency_le_fifteen_mul_packing_add_surplus
declarations.type:

  (∀ (vertex : V), 3 ≤ object.degree vertex) →
    (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.remainderDeficiencyProfile object).positiveDeficiency ≤
      15 * StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.packingNumber object +
        StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.windowSurplus object

declarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.remainderPositiveDeficiency_le_tokenCount
declarations.type:

  (∀ (vertex : V), 3 ≤ object.degree vertex) →
    (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.remainderDeficiencyProfile object).positiveDeficiency ≤
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.tokenCount object

declarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.remainderPositiveDeficiency_sub_remainderSurplus_le
declarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V),
  (∀ (vertex : V), 3 ≤ object.degree vertex) →
    (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.remainderDeficiencyProfile object).positiveDeficiency -
        StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.remainderSurplus object ≤
      15 * StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.packingNumber object +
          StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.windowSurplus object -
        StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.remainderSurplus object

declarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.tokenCount_eq_fifteen_mul_packing_add_surplus
declarations.type:

      15 * StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.packingNumber object +
        StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.windowSurplus object

declarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathWindowLedger.window_add_remainder_eq_totalSurplus

                StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.InternalBaseline boundaries input.minimumDegree

StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression.run_terminal
StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression.run_total
declarations.type:
∀ {input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput} {T : Type u} {boundaries : FinEnum T}
  [inst : Nonempty T] {ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext input.problem input.Target}
  {atom :
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.ProperAtom input boundaries ctx}
  (compression :
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression input boundaries atom),
  ∃ result,
    result.terminal = StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.Terminal.compression ∧
      result.trace =
        [StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.vectorComputation,

          StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.compressionTerminal]
